Ultrasound accuracy & redating — ACOG/AIUM/SMFM

Typical accuracy by trimester

  • 1st trimester (CRL up to 13w6d): most accurate for dating; often within ±5–7 days. (ACOG CO 700)
  • 2nd trimester (BPD/HC/AC/FL): accuracy decreases; roughly ±7–14 days as GA advances. (ACOG CO 700)
  • 3rd trimester: least accurate; around ±21 days; use caution—discrepancy may reflect growth restriction. (ACOG CO 700)

When to redate (replace LMP-based EDD with US-based EDD)

1st trimester (CRL)
  • ≤ 8w6d: redate if GA difference is > 5 days
  • 9w0d–13w6d: redate if GA difference is > 7 days
2nd trimester (BPD/HC/AC/FL)
  • 14w0d–15w6d: redate if > 7 days
  • 16w0d–21w6d: redate if > 10 days
  • 22w0d–27w6d: redate if > 14 days
3rd trimester
  • ≥ 28w0d: redate if > 21 days (use caution—consider growth restriction)
Sources: ACOG/AIUM/SMFM Committee Opinion No. 700 and reputable summaries. See About & Licenses section for links.
Danger Signs in Pregnancy
  • Vaginal bleeding
  • Severe headache, visual disturbances, or convulsions
  • Severe abdominal or lower back pain
  • Swelling of face/hands, shortness of breath
  • Fever, foul-smelling discharge, burning urination
  • Severe vomiting
  • Reduced fetal movements
  • Watery leakage (possible ruptured membranes)
  • Any alarming or worsening symptom

Source: WHO guidelines and Ethiopian national protocols.

Danger Signs in Pregnancy
  • Vaginal bleeding
  • Severe headache, visual disturbances, or convulsions
  • Severe abdominal or lower back pain
  • Swelling of face/hands, shortness of breath
  • Fever, foul-smelling discharge, burning urination
  • Severe vomiting
  • Reduced fetal movements
  • Watery leakage (possible ruptured membranes)
  • Any alarming or worsening symptom
Fetal Milestones — ACOG, WHO & RCOG
  • Weeks 1–8 (1st trimester): Foundational organ formation; cardiac activity appears near the end of the first month. ACOG FAQ156.
  • Weeks 9–12: Major organs and body systems continue developing; fetal movement begins though not yet felt by the mother. ACOG FAQ156.
  • Weeks 13–16: Skeleton continues ossifying; coordinated movements increase. ACOG FAQ156.
  • Weeks 16–20: Maternal perception of movement (quickening) often between 16–20 weeks (later for first pregnancies). RCOG patient info on fetal movements.
  • Weeks 21–24: Ongoing maturation; second‑trimester ultrasound (18–24w) for anatomic survey & dating confirmation. WHO ANC 2016; WHO ultrasound update 2022.
  • ~28 weeks: Brain resting‑state networks mostly local; reflex responses to innocuous stimuli measurable ≈28w. RCOG Fetal Awareness 2022.
  • ~30+ weeks: Long‑range functional connectivity increases; movements organize with sleep–wake cycles. RCOG Fetal Awareness 2022; RCOG patient info.
  • 33 weeks: Differentiated facial & brain responses to noxious vs innocuous stimuli first observed. RCOG Fetal Awareness 2022.
  • Term (37–40+ weeks): Movement should continue up to and during labour; report any reduction promptly. RCOG patient info.
References:
  1. American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists (ACOG). How Your Fetus Grows During Pregnancy (FAQ156, last reviewed Jan 2024). Link.
  2. World Health Organization. WHO recommendations on antenatal care for a positive pregnancy experience (2016); and Maternal and fetal assessment update: imaging ultrasound before 24 weeks of pregnancy (2022). Guideline · Ultrasound update.
  3. Royal College of Obstetricians and Gynaecologists (RCOG). Fetal Awareness: Updated review of Research and Recommendations for Practice (Dec 2022). Link.
  4. RCOG. Your baby’s movements in pregnancy (2019). Link.
